name: CAGR Calculator
description: Calculate compound annual growth rate for investments.

example.explanation: An investment growing from ₹50,000 to ₹1,20,000 in 5 years has a CAGR of 19.17%.

faqs.question: Why use CAGR?
faqs.answer: CAGR smooths out volatility to show the average annual growth rate.

faqs.question: Is it actual return?
faqs.answer: It is a theoretical rate and does not reflect year-to-year variation.

faqs.question: How is CAGR useful?
faqs.answer: It helps compare investment performance across different time periods fairly.
